input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML — use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="24"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#F0F0F0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The `Light` and `Dark` values are applied automatically when the OS theme changes — no event wiring needed.

---

**C# equivalent — use `SetAppTheme` extension method:**

```csharp
var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 24 };
label.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Colors.White);

var button = new Button { Text = "Click Me" };
button.SetAppTheme(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"), Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
button.SetAppTheme(Button.TextColorProperty, Colors.White, Colors.Black);

this.SetAppTheme(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White, Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));
```

Signature: `SetAppTheme(BindableProperty, lightValue, darkValue)`

---

**Key points:**

- Reacts to `Application.RequestedTheme` changes at runtime — no restart required.
- For larger apps, define theme colors in `ResourceDictionary` and reference them with `{StaticResource}` or `{DynamicResource}` instead of inline hex values.
- To force a specific theme regardless of system setting, set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;`.
